# bowtie_ipv4_range (Resource)

Manage an organization IPv4 address pool.

**Note**: per-site routing strategies (`site_strategies`) are not yet managed by this resource. Strategies configured in the Control Plane are preserved across updates and are not cleared.

Destroying this resource deletes the range with Controller cascade behavior enabled so allocations from the pool do not block deletion.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Destroying this resource uses the Controller's cascade delete behavior so
# existing allocations from the pool do not block deletion.
resource "bowtie_ipv4_range" "lan" {
  range                      = "192.0.2.0/24"
  assign_addresses_from_here = "always-assign-random"
  skip_first_n_addresses     = 1
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `assign_addresses_from_here` (String) Address assignment strategy: `never`, `on-demand-assign-random`, or `always-assign-random`.
- `range` (String) The IPv4 CIDR for the pool, for example `192.0.2.0/24`.

### Optional

- `skip_first_n_addresses` (Number) Number of addresses to skip at the start of the range before assigning.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.
- `last_updated` (String) Provider metadata: the last time Terraform changed this object.
